items.title: Confidently wrong is worse than "I don't know"
items.description:

items.title: Sparse KV Caches Cut Attention Scaling
items.description: Sparse key‑value caches collapse the quadratic blow‑up of softmax attention into a cost that grows near‑linearly with sequence length. By making each query attend to a tiny, top‑k subset of blockwise KV memories, the per‑query work stops scaling with the full context. This tiny change flips the s
